title: Micro Dungeon Crawl Travel Pack - How Many Games Can I Fit in the One Card Dungeon box?
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iCV7kvzQ_bA

Daniel (@dungeondive) sets himself a fun challenge ahead of a Disneyland trip: how many complete solo games can he cram into the playing-card-sized box of One Card Dungeon? The answer is five — and he tours each. One Card Dungeon itself is a tactical dice-placement puzzle crawl across twelve levels (range and movement counted in movement-points, a tight action economy, pick-a-power heroes); he finds it genuinely hard but skilful. Tin Helm, Jason Glover's micro-quest, packs astonishing game into a tiny footprint. Business Card Dungeon Dive (by Mel) is a near-complete solo RPG squeezed onto a single card — wilderness and dungeon sides, quests, combat, the lot. And CC Chamberlain's Advanced Quest (the New Hill and Among the Fungi modules) builds a custom dice-pool character and rolls through a branching, chart-driven story.

Everything — five games, two sets of dice, even room for a pen — fits in one inch-thick box. He muses honestly whether he'd take this or his game-stuffed Nintendo Switch, landing on "it depends" (the box wins for camping or no-charger trips).

A celebration of how much modern micro-games deliver, and a blueprint anyone can copy for a travel dungeon kit.

----

What would *you* pack in a playing-card-sized box of solo games — and does a micro travel kit ever beat just loading up your phone?
